methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
ABL Bio is a public-stage biotech company (KOSDAQ-listed) that has raised over $5 billion in aggregate proceeds including IPO and licensing transactions. The valuation universe comprises 11 comparables in the Biotech & Health sector: 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies including Tempus AI, Abridge, Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, and Freenome.

Two standard valuation methods were evaluated. Peer-Median multiples could not be applied due to insufficient narrow comparables; while 16 same-sector peers exist, filtering by stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints. Public-Comp Anchoring using price-to-sales multiples is inapplicable because ABL Bio's revenue is not disclosed—a common limitation for private-to-public transitions in biotech, where licensing and upfront payments may obscure normalized operating metrics. The sector P/S median across the three public tickers stands at 7.76x, but cannot be applied without the target's revenue baseline.

Given the absence of directly applicable quantitative methods, this analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range. A complete assessment would require either (1) disclosure of ABL Bio's revenue or comparable operating metrics, (2) identification of additional same-stage or adjacent-stage peers meeting materiality thresholds, or (3) transaction multiples from recent financings in the private peer set. Key uncertainties include revenue recognition treatment post-IPO and the comparability of licensing economics across the comp universe.
